_comment: Base Rowboat config template. deploy-client.sh writes the live .env with real values. The llm-router sidecar routes gemini-* traffic to Google and llama*/qwen* traffic to Ollama, so a single Rowboat can run both agents below.

agents.description: Primary agent for inbound SMS. Handles greetings, FAQs, and lead qualification. Ollama-backed for cost and latency.

Proxy agent for voice tool calls originating from the Telnyx <-> Gemini Live media bridge. Runs on Gemini so it can reason over the full vault and emit structured tool invocations. The bridge calls this agent's /chat endpoint; Rowboat in turn posts to the platform Next.js /api/voice/tools/* adapters for Nango / Telnyx / CRM work.
Catch-all for SMS-initiated delegations (CRM updates, browser searches, owner-defined HTTP integrations). Uses the SMS tier Ollama model so we don't burn Gemini tokens on background work.

llm_router.note: Internal docker-compose service alias. llm-router forwards gemini-* to Google's OpenAI-compat endpoint and everything else to Ollama on the host.

voice.note: Inbound PSTN voice is handled by Supabase Edge (Telnyx webhooks) and the VPS media bridge. The bridge posts tool calls to /api/voice/tools/* on the platform; Rowboat's voice_task agent is available as a fallback when a tool response needs additional reasoning.

tools.name: http_api_call
tools.description: Call an owner-registered REST API (custom integration) without ever seeing the credential. The owner manages these in /dashboard/integrations → Custom integrations. Pick by `label`. The platform proxy injects the credential server-side and forwards the response.

webhook._comment_url: businessId is pinned via URL query at deploy time so the model has no input surface to influence which tenant's credentials get used. {{BUSINESS_ID}} is substituted from the per-VPS env at workflow seed time (deploy-client.sh). DO NOT move businessId into input_schema or the JSON body.

label.description: Owner's label for the custom integration (case-insensitive, e.g. 'Acme CRM').

path.description: Path appended to the integration's base URL. Must start with '/'.

body.description: Request body (object or array; serialized as JSON unless contentType overrides).

headers.description: Extra outbound headers. Authorization/Cookie/Host are stripped server-side.
